INT. LIBRARY - NIGHT

Rain hammers the windows. The library is dark except for a single desk lamp casting a warm circle of light.

ANNA sits surrounded by stacks of old books. Her fingers are stained with ink. She carefully turns the page of a leather-bound journal dated 1887.

She reads aloud, barely above a whisper.

ANNA
(reading)
"The third stone must be placed at the center. Not before. Not after. The alignment occurs once every thirteen years."

She looks up. Stares at the ceiling. Processes the information. Then she flips to the next page. The handwriting becomes erratic, rushed.

ANNA
(reading)
"If you are reading this, you are already too late. The door opens from the other side. There is no key. There is only—"

The page is torn. The rest of the sentence is missing.

ANNA
(whispering)
There is only what?

She sets the journal down. Rubs her temples. Looks at the clock — 11:47 PM.

She pulls out her phone. Dials.

ANNA
(into phone)
Hey. I found something. The journal from 1887 — it mentions a third stone. And a door. But the page is torn and I can't— 

VOICE ON PHONE (O.S.)
Anna, it's almost midnight.

ANNA
I know what time it is. This can't wait.

VOICE ON PHONE (O.S.)
What do you need?

ANNA
The archives. Section C, shelf fourteen. There should be a matching volume.

VOICE ON PHONE (O.S.)
I'll check tomorrow morning.

ANNA
Tonight. It has to be tonight.

A long pause on the other end.

VOICE ON PHONE (O.S.)
I'll call you back.

ANNA hangs up. She stares at the journal. Then she stands, walks to the window, and looks out at the rain-slicked street below.

A figure stands across the street, under a streetlight. Motionless. Watching the library.

ANNA steps back from the window. Her heart races. She grabs the journal and shoves it into her bag.

She turns off the desk lamp. The room plunges into darkness. Only the rain and her breathing.

She moves to the door. Hesitates. Then opens it slowly. The hallway is empty.

She tiptoes down the corridor, past rows of sleeping shelves. Her phone buzzes. She checks it.

TEXT FROM UNKNOWN: "We know you have the journal."

ANNA's blood runs cold. She pockets the phone and moves faster.

She reaches the back exit. Pushes the door. It's locked. She searches her bag for keys. Her hands shake.

The sound of footsteps behind her. Slow. Deliberate.

ANNA finds her keys. Fumbles them into the lock. The door opens. She steps out into the rain.

She runs. The figure from across the street is gone. But someone — or something — is following her through the rain.

FADE OUT.
